name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2
description: Aligns fully with NIST SP 800-171, introducing 110 controls for companies handling CUI data.

questions.name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 - Authorized Logical Access Control
questions.text: Are system access rights limited to authorized users, processes, or devices (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Review user access provisioning logs, check active directory profiles, and audit router access lists.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Maintain alignment with NIST SP 800-171/172 assessment scoring rules for federal defense contracting.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the access control domain configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Assessment evidence must include: System Security Plan (SSP), Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M), NIST SP 800-171/172 DoD Assessment Score register, and CMMC Certified Third-Party Assessment Organization (C3PAO) audit traces.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical CMMC L2 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 - Authorized External Connections
questions.text: Are external logical connections authorized, documented, and monitored (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?

1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.

4. Maintain alignment with NIST SP 800-171/172 assessment scoring rules for federal defense contracting.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the access control domain configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Assessment evidence must include: System Security Plan (SSP), Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M), NIST SP 800-171/172 DoD Assessment Score register, and CMMC Certified Third-Party Assessment Organization (C3PAO) audit traces.

questions.name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 - Unique User Identification
questions.text: Are all human users uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing the system?
questions.description:
Ensure each employee has unique login credentials and default accounts are completely disabled.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Maintain alignment with NIST SP 800-171/172 assessment scoring rules for federal defense contracting.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the identification domain configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Assessment evidence must include: System Security Plan (SSP), Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M), NIST SP 800-171/172 DoD Assessment Score register, and CMMC Certified Third-Party Assessment Organization (C3PAO) audit traces.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 - Password Complexity Baselines
questions.text: Are password complexity standards and active lockouts enforced?
questions.description:
Verify AD GPO password rules, check lockout thresholds, and audit password expiration parameters.

1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.

questions.name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 - Physical Media Sanitization
questions.text: Is media containing Controlled Unclassified Information sanitized prior to disposal?
questions.description:
Review media degaussing and destruction procedures, inspect disposal logs, and check third-party sanitation certificates.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Maintain alignment with NIST SP 800-171/172 assessment scoring rules for federal defense contracting.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the media protection domain configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Assessment evidence must include: System Security Plan (SSP), Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M), NIST SP 800-171/172 DoD Assessment Score register, and CMMC Certified Third-Party Assessment Organization (C3PAO) audit traces.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Maintain alignment with NIST SP 800-171/172 assessment scoring rules for federal defense contracting.

questions.name: CMMC 2.0 Level 2 - Identify and Correct Flaws
questions.text: Are system software security flaws identified, reported, and corrected in a timely manner (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Verify patch schedules, review automated scanner outputs, and check vulnerability response trackers.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Maintain alignment with NIST SP 800-171/172 assessment scoring rules for federal defense contracting.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the system & info integrity configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Assessment evidence must include: System Security Plan (SSP), Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M), NIST SP 800-171/172 DoD Assessment Score register, and CMMC Certified Third-Party Assessment Organization (C3PAO) audit traces.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.
